questions.text: According to the ACFE, what is the primary defining characteristic of a fictitious vendor scheme in the context of disbursement fraud?
options: A. The fraudster creates false invoices from an existing legitimate vendor to inflate costs
B. The fraudster creates a completely fabricated vendor entity and submits invoices for goods or services never received
C. The fraudster intercepts legitimate vendor payments and redirects them to personal accounts
D. The fraudster modifies vendor bank account information in the accounting system to receive payments
questions.tag: Fictitious Vendor Schemes — Definition

questions.text: Which of the following best defines a 'duplicate payment' scheme in disbursement fraud?
options: A. Processing the same invoice twice intentionally to create two separate payments for identical goods
B. Creating multiple invoices from different vendors for the same product or service
C. Altering vendor invoices to show different amounts and submitting both versions for payment
D. Paying a vendor invoice through both the accounts payable system and the petty cash system simultaneously
questions.tag: Duplicate Payment Schemes — Concept Definition

questions.text: In the context of ACFE fraud classification, what is a 'skimming scheme' as distinct from other disbursement frauds?
options: A. The removal of cash from the organization before it is recorded in the accounting system
B. The alteration of recorded disbursements to divert funds to unauthorized recipients
C. The creation of fraudulent vendor accounts to process unauthorized payments
D. The manipulation of expense reimbursement documentation to obtain excessive payments
questions.tag: Skimming vs. Disbursement Fraud — Distinction

questions.text: According to ACFE standards, what is the defining element of a 'lapping scheme' in relation to receivables fraud?
options: A. The delay of cash application and use of subsequent customer payments to cover earlier theft
B. The creation of duplicate customer accounts to process multiple transactions
C. The alteration of customer payment amounts in the accounting records
D. The interception of customer checks before they reach the organization
questions.tag: Lapping Schemes — Receivables-Related Concept

questions.text: What is the primary distinction between an 'authorized disbursement' fraud and an 'unauthorized disbursement' fraud in the ACFE taxonomy?
options: A. Authorized fraud uses forged documents while unauthorized fraud uses legitimate documents
B. Authorized fraud involves approval within established authorization limits while unauthorized fraud exceeds or circumvents authorization protocols
C. Authorized fraud targets cash only while unauthorized fraud involves all asset types
D. Authorized fraud requires management collusion while unauthorized fraud can be perpetrated by a single employee
questions.tag: Authorization Framework — Fraud Classification

questions.text: In ACFE terminology, what is a 'side agreement' in the context of vendor fraud schemes?
options: A. A verbal understanding between the fraudster and a vendor to inflate invoice amounts
B. A supplementary understanding or contract that contradicts the stated terms of the primary purchase agreement
C. An informal document that modifies official purchase orders without proper authorization
D. A vendor arrangement that allows for payment terms longer than standard company policy
questions.tag: Vendor Collusion Concepts — Side Agreements

questions.text: According to ACFE fraud examination standards, what is the core definition of an 'overbilling scheme'?
options: A. Submitting invoices for services rendered but charging amounts significantly above market rates
B. Invoicing for goods or services in quantities greater than actually delivered or performed
C. Creating invoices from vendors that do not exist in the organization's vendor database
D. Charging customers multiple times for the same transaction to generate additional revenue
questions.tag: Overbilling Schemes — Definition and Scope

questions.text: What is the distinguishing characteristic of a 'kickback scheme' in the ACFE fraud classification system?
options: A. The fraudster creates false invoices to justify unauthorized disbursements
B. A vendor receives inflated payments and returns a portion of the overpayment to the fraudster or an accomplice
C. An employee submits personal expenses as business expenses for reimbursement
D. The fraudster intercepts payments intended for legitimate vendors and deposits them into personal accounts
questions.tag: Kickback Schemes — Vendor Collusion Definition

questions.text: In the ACFE framework, what is meant by 'approval fraud' in disbursement transactions?
options: A. The fraudster forges the signature of an authorized approver on disbursement documents
B. The fraudster obtains legitimate approval for fraudulent disbursements by misrepresenting the nature of the transaction
C. The fraudster submits transactions below the approval threshold to avoid management review
D. The fraudster manipulates the vendor approval list to include unauthorized entities
questions.tag: Approval Fraud — Authorization Manipulation

questions.text: According to ACFE terminology, what is a 'short-pay scheme' in the context of vendor fraud?
options: A. Intentionally underpaying a vendor invoice to divert the difference to personal accounts
B. Paying only a portion of a legitimate invoice and falsifying records to show full payment
C. Submitting invoices for partial deliveries while charging for complete orders
D. Reducing invoice amounts in the accounting system without corresponding vendor authorization
questions.tag: Short-Pay Schemes — Vendor Payment Manipulation

questions.text: What is the primary defining characteristic of a 'reverse billing' scheme according to ACFE standards?
options: A. The fraudster submits credit memos from vendors that were never actually issued
B. The organization is billed by a vendor for goods that were actually returned or never received
C. The fraudster creates invoices where the organization appears as the vendor rather than the customer
D. A vendor refunds payments for legitimate transactions to facilitate money laundering
questions.tag: Reverse Billing Schemes — Alternative Vendor Fraud Type

questions.text: In ACFE terminology, what does the term 'account manipulation' refer to in disbursement fraud?
options: A. The unauthorized alteration of vendor account information to redirect payments
B. The creation of temporary accounts in the general ledger to hide fraudulent transactions
C. The intentional misclassification of disbursement transactions to different accounts to conceal fraud
D. The unauthorized modification of employee bank account details on payroll systems
questions.tag: Account Manipulation — Recording Concealment Technique

questions.text: According to ACFE standards, what is the defining element that separates 'expense reimbursement fraud' from other disbursement schemes?
options: A. It involves the falsification of vendor invoices to justify payments
B. It requires collusion between an employee and external vendor accomplices
C. An employee falsifies or misrepresents personal expenses as legitimate business expenses to obtain reimbursement
D. It involves the manipulation of purchase orders before goods are received
questions.tag: Expense Reimbursement Fraud — Employee-Initiated Disbursement

questions.text: What is the primary distinction between a lapping scheme and a skimming scheme in the context of cash disbursements fraud?
options: A. Lapping involves the use of company checks while skimming involves cash; both occur at the point of sale
B. Lapping uses customer payments to conceal prior thefts while skimming removes cash before it is recorded in the accounting system
C. Lapping occurs in accounts payable while skimming occurs exclusively in accounts receivable
D. Lapping requires management approval while skimming can be perpetrated by any employee with cash access
questions.tag: Cash Handling Schemes — Lapping vs. Skimming Distinction

questions.text: Which concept best differentiates a fictitious vendor scheme from a shell company scheme in billing fraud?
options: A. A fictitious vendor is completely nonexistent while a shell company is an actual entity registered but inactive
B. A fictitious vendor requires falsified invoices while a shell company only requires legitimate invoices from a legitimate entity
C. A fictitious vendor can only be used once while a shell company can submit multiple invoices over time
D. A fictitious vendor scheme requires collusion while a shell company scheme can be perpetrated by a single employee
questions.tag: Billing Schemes — Fictitious Vendor vs. Shell Company

questions.text: In the context of check tampering schemes, what distinguishes a forged endorsement from a stolen check scheme?
options: A. A forged endorsement modifies the payee name while a stolen check involves unauthorized alteration of the amount only
B. A forged endorsement allows the fraudster to cash a check intended for another party while a stolen check is simply cashed as-is by the perpetrator
C. A forged endorsement requires the original check maker's knowledge while a stolen check does not
D. A forged endorsement is less detectable because the check appears legitimate while a stolen check is immediately flagged by the bank
questions.tag: Check Tampering — Forged Endorsement vs. Stolen Check

questions.text: What is the key operational difference between an authorized maker scheme and a forged maker scheme in check fraud?
options: A. An authorized maker scheme uses legitimate signatories while a forged maker scheme uses counterfeit signatures without authorization
B. An authorized maker scheme requires embezzlement skills while a forged maker scheme requires document forgery expertise
C. An authorized maker scheme is detected immediately by reconciliation while a forged maker scheme may evade detection longer
D. An authorized maker scheme involves checks written to legitimate vendors while a forged maker scheme involves checks to personal accounts only
questions.tag: Check Tampering — Authorized vs. Forged Maker Schemes

questions.text: How does a duplicate invoice billing scheme differ from an overbilling scheme in terms of method and detection?
options: A. A duplicate invoice involves submitting the same invoice multiple times while overbilling inflates unit prices or quantities on legitimate invoices
B. A duplicate invoice is easier to detect because it has identical amounts while overbilling is harder to detect because amounts vary
C. A duplicate invoice requires collusion with accounting while overbilling can be perpetrated by a single employee
D. A duplicate invoice only occurs in vendor fraud while overbilling occurs in all billing schemes
questions.tag: Billing Schemes — Duplicate Invoice vs. Overbilling

questions.text: Which aspect most clearly distinguishes a collusive vendor fraud scheme from a non-collusive one in the accounts payable environment?
options: A. Collusive schemes involve external vendors while non-collusive schemes involve only internal employees
B. Collusive schemes require an actual vendor involved in the fraudulent scheme while non-collusive schemes can be perpetrated using fictitious vendors
C. Collusive schemes are always detected first by internal audit while non-collusive schemes are detected by external auditors
D. Collusive schemes involve larger dollar amounts while non-collusive schemes involve smaller, repeated thefts
questions.tag: Vendor Fraud — Collusive vs. Non-Collusive Schemes

questions.text: What differentiates a salary advance fraud from a falsified expense reimbursement in the context of payroll and disbursements?
options: A. A salary advance is taken from future earnings while a falsified expense reimbursement is claimed for expenses that never occurred
B. A salary advance requires documented board approval while a falsified expense reimbursement only requires employee approval
C. A salary advance affects the payroll system while a falsified expense reimbursement affects the accounts payable system
D. A salary advance is recorded in payroll deductions while a falsified expense reimbursement is never recorded in any system
questions.tag: Disbursement Fraud Types — Salary Advances vs. Expense Reimbursements

questions.text: In comparing altered payee checks to blank check fraud, which distinction is most significant from an investigative perspective?
options: A. Altered payee checks involve changing an existing check while blank checks are completely unsigned and unwritten
B. Altered payee checks require the fraudster to possess the original check while blank checks only require knowledge of the check stock location
C. Altered payee checks are always caught during bank reconciliation while blank checks may evade detection if check numbering is not sequential
D. Altered payee checks involve check washing while blank checks involve check forgery techniques only
questions.tag: Check Tampering — Altered Payee vs. Blank Check Schemes

questions.text: What fundamentally distinguishes a false refund scheme from a personal purchases billing scheme in disbursements fraud?
options: A. A false refund involves returning nonexistent products while a personal purchases scheme bills personal items as business expenses
B. A false refund requires merchandise to be involved while a personal purchases scheme can involve services or intangible items
C. A false refund is initiated by the fraudster while a personal purchases scheme requires customer initiation
D. A false refund is recorded in accounts receivable while a personal purchases scheme is recorded in accounts payable
questions.tag: Cash Disbursements — False Refund vs. Personal Purchases

questions.text: Which control difference most effectively distinguishes prevention of related-party vendor schemes from prevention of fictitious vendor schemes?
options: A. Related-party schemes require segregation of duties while fictitious vendor schemes require supplier verification

B. Related-party schemes are prevented by disclosure requirements and conflict-of-interest policies while fictitious vendor schemes are prevented by vendor master file controls and independent verification
C. Related-party schemes need competitive bidding procedures while fictitious vendor schemes need only invoice review
D. Related-party schemes must be monitored by internal audit while fictitious vendor schemes can be controlled by accounts payable clerks
questions.tag: Vendor Fraud Prevention — Related-Party vs. Fictitious Vendor Controls

questions.text: In expense reimbursement fraud, what differentiates a submitted false receipt scheme from a mischaracterized expense scheme?
options: A. A false receipt scheme uses completely fabricated documentation while a mischaracterized expense scheme uses legitimate receipts but codes them to the wrong cost center
B. A false receipt scheme requires digital document manipulation while a mischaracterized expense scheme only requires incorrect reporting
C. A false receipt scheme involves personal expenses while a mischaracterized expense scheme involves only business expenses
D. A false receipt scheme is easier to detect than mischaracterized expenses due to payment verification
questions.tag: Expense Reimbursement Fraud — False Receipts vs. Mischaracterized Expenses

questions.text: What key operational difference exists between a round-tripping scheme and a pay-and-return scheme in vendor fraud?
options: A. Round-tripping involves multiple vendors while pay-and-return involves a single vendor relationship
B. Round-tripping cycles money through multiple entities to launder funds while pay-and-return involves paying a vendor then receiving the money back as a refund or return
C. Round-tripping requires international transactions while pay-and-return occurs only domestically
D. Round-tripping is collusive while pay-and-return is always non-collusive
questions.tag: Vendor Fraud Schemes — Round-Tripping vs. Pay-and-Return

questions.text: Which factor most clearly distinguishes a check kiting scheme from other check tampering disbursement frauds in a banking context?
options: A. Check kiting involves creating a check while other schemes involve altering existing checks
B. Check kiting exploits the float period between bank accounts to create temporary false balances while other check schemes directly steal funds or assets
C. Check kiting requires coordination with bank employees while other schemes do not
D. Check kiting involves only two accounts while other schemes can involve multiple accounts
questions.tag: Check Tampering — Check Kiting vs. Other Check Schemes

questions.text: An examiner discovers that a purchasing manager has been submitting invoices from a vendor owned by the manager's spouse. The vendor's prices are 15-20% above market rate, and deliveries are often incomplete. Which red flag is most concerning in detecting this scheme?
options: A. The incomplete deliveries suggest quality control failures unrelated to fraud
B. The related-party relationship combined with overpricing indicates potential collusive fraud
C. The 15-20% price variance is within acceptable industry fluctuation margins
D. The use of a legitimate vendor account masks the true nature of the transaction
questions.tag: Vendor Fraud — Related-Party Collusion Detection

questions.text: During a disbursement audit, a CFE notices that checks are being issued to 'Cash' rather than to specific vendors, with no supporting purchase orders. The amounts range from $2,000 to $8,000. What is the primary fraud scheme indicated by these characteristics?
options: A. Shell company billing scheme
B. Unauthorized cash disbursement scheme
C. Duplicate payment fraud
D. Personal expense reimbursement scheme
questions.tag: Cash Disbursements — Unauthorized Payments

questions.text: An AP clerk processes invoices for office supplies from three different vendors that all share the same physical address and telephone number, but use different business names. What investigative step should the examiner prioritize?
options: A. Request payment history to identify timing patterns of invoices
B. Verify the vendors' business registrations and beneficial ownership through public records
C. Compare invoice formats, fonts, and paper quality across all three vendors
D. Contact the vendors directly using phone numbers listed on their invoices
questions.tag: Fictitious Vendor Scheme — Entity Verification

questions.text: A CFE reviewing expense reimbursements identifies an employee who submitted meal receipts totaling $18,500 over six months, all from high-end restaurants, with numerous instances where the receipt date does not match the employee's recorded business travel dates. Which control would most effectively prevent this type of fraud going forward?
options: A. Implementing a monthly expense cap of $3,000 regardless of justification
B. Requiring real-time geolocation data from the employee's mobile device
C. Establishing a three-way match between travel authorization, receipt date, and business purpose documentation
D. Limiting reimbursable meal expenses to company-contracted vendors only
questions.tag: Expense Reimbursement Fraud — Temporal Misalignment Detection

questions.text: An accounts payable analyst notices that a vendor invoice for $4,800 was paid twice within 10 days, using different check numbers but the same invoice number and amount. When the second payment was made, the vendor's account showed no credit balance or dispute notation. What is the most likely explanation?
options: A. The vendor intentionally submitted duplicate invoices to test the company's controls
B. A system error automatically reprocessed the invoice after the first payment cleared
C. An employee deliberately submitted the invoice twice to create a fraudulent payment opportunity
D. The purchasing department requested expedited payment without notifying accounts payable
questions.tag: Duplicate Payment Fraud — Preventive Control Assessment

questions.text: During an investigation, a CFE discovers that a warehouse manager has been receiving kickbacks from a shipping contractor. The contractor invoices the company for services at inflated rates, then pays the manager 10% of the overbilled amount in cash. Which internal control would be most effective at detecting this scheme?
options: A. Requiring competitive bids from at least three contractors before contract award
B. Implementing surprise audits of shipping invoices against actual services rendered and market rates
C. Establishing a policy prohibiting employees from accepting gifts from vendors
D. Creating a segregation of duties between shipping invoice authorization and payment processing
questions.tag: Kickback Schemes — Detection Through Price Verification

questions.text: An examiner finds that an employee in the receiving department has authority to sign off on receiving reports for inventory up to 50 items without supervisory review. This same employee also approves invoices for payment. Last month, the employee processed a receiving report for 500 units of computer equipment but only 250 units were delivered. The invoice was approved and paid in full. What fundamental control breakdown is evident?
options: A. Lack of segregation of duties between receiving, authorization, and payment functions
B. Absence of periodic physical inventory counts to verify recorded quantities
C. Inadequate training for receiving staff on proper documentation procedures
D. Insufficient vendor selection criteria to identify unreliable suppliers
questions.tag: Inventory Falsification — Receiving-to-Payment Control Deficiency

questions.text: A CFE reviewing check register details notices that payments to a particular vendor are consistently made two days before the invoice date appears in the accounting system. When the examiner requests supporting documentation, the invoices are dated after the payment date. What red flag is most significant?
options: A. The timing suggests a data entry error in the accounts payable system
B. The payment predating the invoice indicates potential invoice manipulation or unauthorized early payments
C. The delay is likely due to postal delays in invoice delivery
D. The vendor's billing procedures are unusual but not indicative of fraud
questions.tag: Invoice Fraud — Temporal Inconsistencies

questions.text: An employee with authority to approve travel advances up to $2,000 has been submitting multiple advance requests of $1,900 each for the same business trip, spacing them three days apart. After receiving the advances, the employee delays submitting expense reports, and the advances are not properly reconciled against actual expenses. Which preventive control is most critical?
options: A. Requiring advance requests to be approved only by the CFO
B. Implementing a system control that aggregates all advances per employee per trip and enforces a combined limit
C. Establishing a policy that travel advances cannot exceed 50% of estimated trip costs
D. Requiring personal guarantees signed by the employee acknowledging repayment obligations
questions.tag: Advance Abuse — Circumvention Through Fragmentation

questions.text: During a fraud investigation, it is discovered that a procurement officer has been issuing purchase orders to a vendor for services that were never delivered or rendered. The vendor's owner is a personal friend of the procurement officer. Bank records show the fraudster received regular transfers from the vendor's business account to a personal account. Over 18 months, the scheme generated $340,000 in fraudulent payments. What is the PRIMARY missing control that allowed this scheme to persist?
options: A. A requirement for competitive bidding on all purchases above a specified threshold
B. Independent verification that goods or services were actually received before invoice payment
C. Monthly reconciliation of the accounts payable subsidiary ledger to the general ledger
D. Documented conflict-of-interest policies prohibiting procurement staff from transacting with related parties
questions.tag: Fictitious Service Billing — Receiving Verification Control

questions.text: A CFE examining payroll disbursements discovers that a terminated employee's name remains on the payroll system with an active status. Paychecks are still being issued bi-weekly to an address different from the employee's last known residence. The HR department has no record of the address change. Which explanation is most consistent with payroll fraud?
options: A. The payroll system has a software glitch that prevents proper employee termination processing
B. An employee with access to payroll records is continuing the former employee's pay for personal benefit
C. The employee requested continuous payment to a new address but failed to update HR records
D. The terminated employee is entitled to severance payments under the company's policy
questions.tag: Ghost Employee Schemes — Terminated Employee Status Abuse

questions.text: An AP department has implemented a three-way match control (PO, receiving report, invoice) for all purchases over $5,000. However, a fraud examiner discovers that an employee has been creating purchase orders for non-existent items, receiving reports that falsely confirm delivery, and submitting invoices that pass the three-way match. The fraud totaled $127,000 over eight months. What does this case demonstrate about control effectiveness?
options: A. Three-way match controls are ineffective and should be replaced with vendor audits
B. Preventive controls can be circumvented if the same person participates in all three matching processes
C. Three-way match controls require supplementary detective controls such as periodic physical verification
D. The fraud indicates that purchase order amounts were not properly monitored for reasonableness
questions.tag: Control Design Deficiency — Collusion in Preventive Controls

questions.text: A CFE is investigating a scheme where an employee in accounts payable has been processing invoices for small miscellaneous supplies ($300-$800 each) from a vendor that the employee owns personally. Over two years, the company paid approximately $89,000 for supplies that were never received. The invoices were processed without supporting purchase orders or evidence of delivery. Which combination of controls would have been most effective in preventing this scheme?
options: A. Price ceiling limits and mandatory competitive bidding for all purchases
B. A requirement for all vendors to be pre-approved and listed on an authorized vendor roster, combined with mandatory receiving verification before payment
C. Regular audits of expense categories and a policy requiring annual recertification of vendor information
D. Segregation of duties between purchase order creation and invoice approval, with a secondary review of all purchases by the same employee
questions.tag: Personal Vendor Fraud — Prevention Through Vendor Management and Verification

questions.text: A purchasing manager at a manufacturing company has been systematically splitting large purchase orders into multiple smaller invoices from the same vendor to avoid the $10,000 approval threshold. Each invoice is individually approved by the manager without oversight. Over 18 months, $847,000 in fraudulent purchases were made. Which combination of factors best explains why this scheme persisted undetected?
options: A. The vendor maintained legitimate business operations and the goods were partially received, combined with weak segregation of duties between ordering, approval, and receiving functions
B. The scheme involved only small individual amounts that fell below audit materiality thresholds, and the company lacked any vendor master file reconciliation procedures
C. The manager possessed legitimate purchasing authority and the invoices contained accurate descriptions, making detection impossible without external vendor confirmation
D. The company's accounting system automatically flagged all invoices above $5,000, preventing the manager from exceeding the approval limit
questions.tag: Splitting Purchases — Authority Override

questions.text: During a forensic review, a CFE identifies that an accounts payable employee created duplicate vendor records in the accounting system, with nearly identical names (e.g., 'Acme Supplies Inc.' and 'Acme Supplies, Inc.'). Invoices were systematically routed to both accounts for the same services. Which preventive control would most effectively have mitigated this specific vulnerability?
options: A. Requiring all new vendor setup to include tax identification number verification and periodic vendor master file reconciliation with the general ledger
B. Implementing a policy requiring three-way invoice matching between purchase orders, receiving documents, and vendor invoices
C. Installing a system-based duplicate payment prevention tool that automatically blocks identical invoice amounts within 30 days
D. Conducting quarterly audits of accounts payable to ensure all vendors are legitimate and properly documented
questions.tag: Duplicate Vendor Records — Master File Control

questions.text: An employee in the disbursements department has been intercepting checks intended for legitimate vendors, altering the payee name to a fraudulent entity, and depositing the funds into a personal business account. The scheme went undetected for 14 months across $312,000 in disbursements. Which sequence of control breakdowns best explains this outcome?
options: A. The company failed to implement check-signing authority restrictions, lacked reconciliation of checks issued to actual vendor payments, and did not perform periodic bank reconciliation reviews
B. The fraudster possessed legitimate access to blank check stock and had override authority over the accounts payable system, making any control ineffective
C. The vendor master file was not protected by password controls, allowing the employee to change vendor payment addresses without detection
D. The company used pre-printed check stock with vendor names, making alteration technically impossible, yet the scheme persisted due to a data entry error
questions.tag: Check Alteration — Payee Manipulation

questions.text: A supply chain manager has been submitting invoices from a legitimate vendor for goods that were never received, creating a fictitious receiving report to match each invoice. The scheme involved $425,000 over two years. During investigation, the CFE discovers the manager also approved all receiving reports and had authority to waive receipt requirements for 'emergency orders.' Which conclusion is most supported by these facts?
options: A. The lack of segregation of duties between approval, receipt, and invoice processing, combined with discretionary authority to bypass controls, created an environment where the fraudster could operate with minimal oversight
B. The vendor deliberately conspired with the manager to submit false invoices, which means the company's detection controls were adequate but the external threat was uncontrollable
C. The company's inventory management system should have automatically flagged missing goods, indicating a failed preventive technology control
D. The manager's legitimate emergency order authority rendered all other controls ineffective, making this scheme unpreventable
questions.tag: Fictitious Disbursements — Receipt Documentation Fraud

questions.text: An employee submitted expense reports for reimbursement claiming $8,400 in business meals over four months. Investigation revealed the employee submitted identical receipt amounts on multiple dates, used personal credit card statements with handwritten alterations, and inflated tip amounts. The employee had never had an expense report rejected. Which factor most directly enabled this scheme to persist?
options: A. The expense amounts fell below the $10,000 individual approval threshold and lacked substantive review procedures that would identify duplicate amounts or inconsistent receipt patterns
B. The company's accounting system automatically approves all expense reports submitted before month-end without human review
C. Expense reimbursement policies explicitly permitted employees to estimate meal costs when receipts were unavailable
D. The manager was not trained on fraud detection techniques and therefore could not identify fabricated receipts
questions.tag: Expense Reimbursement Fraud — Receipt Manipulation

questions.text: A procurement specialist created purchase orders and corresponding invoices for a 'consulting firm' that provided no services. The invoices were detailed with project descriptions and timekeeping summaries. Over 22 months, $567,000 was disbursed. The specialist had authority to approve orders up to $15,000 and submitted invoices within this limit. Investigation revealed no bank account segregation between legitimate and fraudulent transactions. Which combination of factors best explains why the fraud progressed to such magnitude?
options: A. The fraudster stayed within individual approval authority limits, created persuasive supporting documentation, and no cumulative vendor analysis or three-way reconciliation was performed to identify the anomalous pattern
B. The company lacked a formal vendor pre-qualification process, allowing the fraudster to register the shell company with minimal documentation
C. The invoices were submitted in small amounts to avoid triggering automated audit flags in the accounting system
D. The bank failed to implement transaction monitoring, allowing all payments to process without institutional verification
questions.tag: Shell Company Scheme — Cumulative Fraud Pattern

questions.text: During a forensic examination, a CFE discovers that a warehouse supervisor has been authorizing payments for 'inventory spoilage write-offs' of $2,000 to $4,000 monthly. These write-offs lacked supporting documentation beyond the supervisor's own inventory count report. Investigation confirms no actual spoilage occurred; funds were diverted to personal use. The supervisor had delegated authority to approve write-offs up to $5,000. Which preventive control would have been most effective?
options: A. Requiring independent physical inventory verification and written approval from a manager outside the warehouse operations function before authorizing any write-off disbursements
B. Installing inventory tracking software that records spoilage automatically based on barcode scanning
C. Implementing a policy limiting write-offs to no more than $1,000 per transaction regardless of authorization level
D. Requiring the supervisor to submit photographic evidence of spoiled goods before any write-off is processed
questions.tag: Unauthorized Write-Offs — Custody and Approval Segregation

questions.text: An HR administrator has been processing payments to terminated employees by recreating their personnel files with modified termination dates. The administrator processed $234,000 in unauthorized payroll over eight months by extending the employment records and issuing checks. The administrator had access to both the payroll system and HR master files without compensating controls. Which conclusion regarding the control environment is most supported?
options: A. The lack of segregation between HR record maintenance and payroll processing, combined with insufficient audit trails and no periodic reconciliation of active employees to payroll records, created vulnerability to this scheme
B. The payroll system should have contained an automated control preventing payments to employees with terminated status, indicating a software design failure
C. The administrator's access level was appropriate given their HR role, meaning management oversight rather than system controls should have prevented this fraud
D. The organization lacked sufficient payroll auditing expertise to implement effective preventive controls
questions.tag: Unauthorized Payroll Disbursements — Record Manipulation

questions.text: A construction company controller approved invoices from a subcontractor that were 15-20% higher than budgeted amounts and prior similar work. Upon investigation, the CFE determines the controller received kickback payments from the subcontractor's owner. The invoices lacked detailed cost breakdowns. The company lacked a formal competitive bidding process for recurring subcontractors. Which factor most directly enabled the controller to perpetrate this scheme without detection?
options: A. The absence of a competitive bidding requirement and detailed price variance analysis for recurring vendors, combined with the controller's unilateral approval authority without oversight, created an environment where inflated invoices could be rationalized
B. The subcontractor deliberately conspired to submit inflated invoices, demonstrating that external fraud cannot be prevented by internal controls
C. The company's industry practice of paying subcontractors at-cost-plus-markup made price verification impossible
D. The controller possessed sole authority over vendor relationships, rendering any audit function irrelevant
questions.tag: Collusive Vendor Fraud — Price Manipulation

questions.text: An IT systems administrator has been processing refund requests for customer returns that were never actually received. The administrator submitted detailed return authorization forms and shipping documentation that appeared legitimate. Over 18 months, $389,000 in unauthorized refunds were processed. Investigation revealed the administrator had access to both the order management system and refund approval authority without supervisory review. Which control design issue most directly contributed to this scheme?
options: A. The complete integration of transaction initiation, approval, and system access within a single person's role without any compensating detective controls or secondary verification of receiving documentation
B. The company's refund policy was too liberal, allowing any employee with system access to process refunds without questioning the legitimacy of returns
C. The shipping and logistics department failed to properly track inbound returned items, creating an information gap
D. The administrator's technical skills enabled them to fabricate system records that were indistinguishable from legitimate transactions
questions.tag: Fictitious Refunds — System Access Consolidation

questions.text: A company discovered that a procurement employee had been submitting invoices for 'equipment maintenance' from a vendor that did not exist. The invoices were formatted professionally and included maintenance schedules. The employee had established a bank account in a similar company name and received the disbursements. The company lacked a vendor prequalification checklist and did not verify vendor legitimacy through business license confirmation. Which sequence of control weaknesses was exploited?
options: A. Absence of vendor verification procedures at registration, lack of periodic vendor legitimacy confirmation, and no bank account verification process when establishing new vendors
B. The vendor master file was not encrypted, allowing the employee to modify vendor payment information without audit trail detection
C. The company should have used only established national vendors with published business licenses for all procurement
D. The employee's job description did not explicitly prohibit creating vendors, indicating a policy gap rather than a control deficiency
questions.tag: Fictitious Vendor Creation — Onboarding Control Failure

questions.text: A finance manager submitted travel reimbursement claims totaling $43,000 over 12 months, claiming business trips that did not occur. The manager submitted airline receipts and hotel invoices that matched legitimate company travel policies. Investigation revealed the manager had printed receipts from third-party websites and altered vendor names. The manager's supervisor approved all travel claims without verifying actual trip occurrence or comparing claims to employee calendars and meeting attendance records. Which conclusion best characterizes the control environment?
options: A. The supervisory review function was performed without substantive verification procedures, relying on policy compliance rather than independent confirmation of trip authenticity or corroborating evidence
B. The company should have required employees to file pre-approved travel requests before any trip, making post-hoc reimbursement impossible
C. The manager's ability to print convincing receipts indicates a detective control failure by the company's receipt verification vendor
D. Travel reimbursement fraud is inherently undetectable because employees naturally have discretion over business trip necessity
questions.tag: Travel Fraud — Supervisory Review Deficiency

questions.text: A purchasing manager colluded with a supplier's sales representative to process invoices for goods at inflated prices. The invoices were submitted monthly in amounts of $8,000-$12,000 and appeared consistent with prior transactions from the same vendor. The excess amounts—approximately $156,000 over 14 months—were split between the manager and the sales representative. The company's approval authority allowed the manager to approve purchases up to $20,000 without secondary review. Analysis of the goods received indicated actual delivery of legitimate merchandise. Which factor most directly prevented detection of this collusive scheme?
options: A. The lack of periodic price verification or competitive bid analysis for ongoing vendors, combined with the absence of management review of total spending patterns with individual vendors, allowed inflated unit prices to persist undetected
B. The company received all ordered goods, making it impossible to detect that prices were inflated because the three-way match reconciliation functioned as designed
C. The sales representative deliberately falsified pricing information at the supplier's accounting level, meaning the fraudster had external accomplices beyond the company's control
D. The manager's approval authority was too high, and reducing the threshold to $5,000 would have prevented all collusive schemes regardless of price inflation
questions.tag: Collusive Price Inflation — Benchmark Analysis Gap
